description: A reed bed that grows back after a flood can be 'thuroi'. The same word fits a community that restores shared services after disruption. What matters is recovery toward a workable state, whether the capacity lies in one thing or in its relationships.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue near the upper teeth for voiceless 'th' and let air pass into rounded high-back 'u'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue against the alveolar ridge for 'r' at the start of stressed 'ro', then continue into rounded mid-back 'o'. Keep final high-front 'i' separate from 'o' in hiatus.
usage_notes: 'mureo' (stable) keeps its state through disturbance; 'luwi' (flexible) changes without breaking. 'thuroi' applies when recovery after disruption is the point.

semantic_domains.physical: return toward workable form or function after stress
